data.text: I found the relevant tests already in `store_test.go`, so this looks like a bounded change rather than a split. I’m reading the current backend-selection path and the checked-in config example to align the implementation with the existing test shape.

data.output:
version: "1.0"
library:
    path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
    repository:
        url: https://github.com/easel/ddx-library
        branch: main
#
# bead_tracker.backend: axon
# bead_tracker:
#   backend: axon
#   axon:
#     endpoint: http://localhost:8080/graphql
#     subscription_endpoint: ws://localhost:8080/graphql
#     database: ddx-default
#     namespace: project-name
#     collection: ddx_beads
#     events_collection: ddx_bead_events
#     schema_version: 1
#     auth:
#       mode: localhost
#       tsnet:
#         hostname: ddx-project
#
# The live config schema currently uses `bead.backend`; this commented block
# documents the Axon backend selection and connection settings for operators.
update_check:
    enabled: true
    frequency: 24h
agent:
    harness: claude
    endpoints:
        - type: lmstudio
          host: vidar
          port: 1234
          api_key: lmstudio
        - type: omlx
          host: vidar
          port: 1235
        - type: lmstudio
          host: bragi
          port: 1234
          api_key: lmstudio
        - type: lmstudio
          host: grendel
          port: 1234
          api_key: lmstudio

require.True(t, ok)
	assert.Equal(t, DefaultCollection, backend.Collection)
	assert.Nil(t, backend.fallback, "default collection must not use fallback")
}

func writeFakeBackendTool(t *testing.T, dir, name string) {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(dir, name)
	script := "#!/bin/sh\nexit 0\n"
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(script), 0o755))
}

func TestCreateAndGet(t *testing.T) {
	s := newTestStore(t)

	b := &Bead{Title: "Fix auth bug", IssueType: "bug", Priority: 1}
	require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))

	assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
	assert.True(t, len(b.ID) > 3, "ID should have prefix + hex")
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", b.IssueType)
	assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, b.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, 1, b.Priority)
	assert.False(t, b.CreatedAt.IsZero())

	got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, b.Title, got.Title)
	assert.Equal(t, b.IssueType, got.IssueType)
}

func TestCreateUsesConfiguredPrefix(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
bead:
  id_prefix: "nif"
`), 0o644))

	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, s.Init())

	b := &Bead{Title: "Configured prefix"}
	require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))

	assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(b.ID, "nif-"))
}

func TestCreateUsesEnvPrefixOverConfig(t *testing.T) {
	tempDir := t.TempDir()
	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
bead:
  id_prefix: "nif"
`), 0o644))

	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "env")

	s := NewStore(filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, s.Init())

	b := &Bead{Title: "Env prefix"}
	require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))

	assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(b.ID, "env-"))
}

func TestCreateDefaults(t *testing.T) {
	s := newTestStore(t)

	b := &Bead{Title: "Simple task"}
	require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))

	assert.Equal(t, DefaultType, b.IssueType)
	assert.Equal(t, DefaultStatus, b.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, b.Priority) // Store does not apply priority defaults; CLI layer sets flag default to 2
	assert.Empty(t, b.Labels)
	assert.Empty(t, b.DepIDs())
}

func TestCreateValidation(t *testing.T) {
	s := newTestStore(t)

	// Empty title
	err := s.Create(&Bead{})
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "title is required")

	// Invalid priority
	err = s.Create(&Bead{Title: "Bad priority", Priority: 9})
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "priority")

	// Invalid status
	err = s.Create(&Bead{Title: "Bad status", Status: "invalid"})
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "invalid status")
}

func TestUpdate(t *testing.T) {
	s := newTestStore(t)

	b := &Bead{Title: "Original"}
	require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))

	err := s.Update(b.ID, func(b *Bead) {
		b.Title = "Updated"
		b.Status = StatusInProgress
		b.Owner = "me"
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "Updated", got.Title)
	assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, "me", got.Owner)
}

func TestUpdateNotFound(t *testing.T) {
	s := newTestStore(t)
	err := s.Update("nonexistent", func(b *Bead) {})
	assert.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "not found")
}

func TestClose(t *testing.T) {
	s := newTestStore(t)

.ddx/executions/20260506T113202-88692371/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.",

3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.

Go resolver tests cover layer filtering, parent/child links, and missing run id (returns nil not error).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-01T02:07:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3396374","closing_commit_sha":"3a064377a28257ab6df4fda1154b9319e6967af0","created_at":"2026-04-30T15:09:59.791736076Z","description":"Add the layer-aware Run type and runs query to schema.graphql, backed by the FEAT-010 file store (.ddx/exec/).

NOTE: Naming distinction — existing ExecutionRun type in schema maps to exec-definition runs (test/check results). The new Run type is the three-layer work/try/run substrate from the agent execute workflow. They are separate types.

Run type fields:
- id, layer (enum: work, try, run), status, startedAt, completedAt
- beadId, artifactId, parentRunId, childRunIds
- Work-layer fields: queueInputs, stopCondition, selectedBeadIds
- Try-layer fields (inline fragment / nullable): beadId, baseRevision, resultRevision, worktreePath, mergeOutcome, checkResults
- Run-layer fields (inline fragment / nullable): promptSummary, powerMin, powerMax, harness, provider, model, tokensIn, tokensOut, costUsd, durationMs, outputExcerpt, evidenceLinks

GraphQL:
- runs(projectID, layer, beadID, status, harness, first, after): RunConnection
- run(id): Run

Resolver reads from .ddx/exec/runs/ (FEAT-010 exec store, file-backed). .ddx/beads.jsonl:555:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing what my agents have done, I open the Executions view, filter to a specific bead or verdict or harness, click into one, and see the prompt it received, the verdict it produced, the session it corresponds to (with cost + tokens), and every tool call it made — all without leaving the browser.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Sidebar entry.** "Executions" added to the project sidebar between "Sessions" and "Personas" (or wherever fits the existing visual order — design call; captured in the PR description). Active-highlight works.

2. **List route.** `/executions` renders a paginated, filterable table with columns: timestamp, bead (id + title as link), harness/model, verdict (color-coded pass/block/error/other), duration, cost. Default sort: newest first. Filters: verdict, harness, bead, since/until date, free-text search over prompt first ~200 chars.

3. **Detail route.** `/executions/<id>` shows five tabs as described (Manifest, Prompt, Result, Session, Tool calls). Tab switching doesn't re-fetch already-loaded data.

4. **Tool calls pane.** Virtualized list. First 50 calls load with the page; subsequent pages load on scroll. Each entry shows tool name + collapsed input/output, expandable on click. Links to the raw agent-log file for operators who want to diff.

5. **GraphQL completeness.** `executions`, `execution`, `executionToolCalls` resolvers exist and pass feat-008-style integration tests. Schema documented in `schema.graphql`.

6. **Cross-links present.**
   - Bead detail renders an "Executions" section listing linked executions with verdict + timestamp, each clicking into the detail route.
   - Session detail shows "Execution" link when the session has one.
   - Commits page shows execution link when commit sha == execution.result_rev.

7. **Fixture + perf.**
   - Seeded fixture: ≥1,000 executions, ≥100 with seeded tool-call streams (≥50 calls each).
   - List view on this fixture: p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Detail view (no tool calls): p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP.
   - Tool-call page 1 (first 50 entries): p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
   - If any target cannot be met in-place, raise DB-substrate decision with user before switching.

8. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a project with 3 executions across 2 beads with mixed verdicts.
   - Navigates to `/executions`, asserts the table renders 3 rows and filter-by-verdict narrows correctly.
   - Clicks into one execution, asserts all 5 tabs exist and the Prompt tab shows the seeded prompt.
   - Opens the Tool calls tab, expands an entry, asserts input/output rendering.
   - Clicks the linked bead from the detail, asserts navigation to bead detail and that the Executions section on that bead lists this execution.

9. **Depends on.**
   - Soft dependency on `ddx-2ceb02fa` for the Session tab's cost/token numbers. If not yet landed, render "not available" for those fields and wire them in after.
   - Adjacent: `ddx-6904a90b` (billing mode) — Session tab's cost shows the correct bucket once that lands.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"59a4ece5923fa29a89addac195937780c28ec955","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:55:34.427486388Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation

`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask "why did this bead end up in this state?" has to drop to the filesystem.

Sessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.

## Ground truth

Per-execution on disk:

```
.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/
  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }
  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent
  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }
```

Relations:
- `manifest.bead_id` → bead
- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata
- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id

## Scope

### Part 1 — GraphQL surface

- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.
- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.
- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.

Executions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.

### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes

- Add "Executions" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).
- Routes:
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.
  - `/nodes/<n>/projects/<p>/executions/<id>` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/<id>`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).

### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)

- **Bead detail:** new "Executions" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.
- **Session detail:** new "Execution" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.
- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.
- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.

### Part 4 — Perf

- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.
- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.
- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.

## Out of scope

- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).
- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).
- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.

=======================

`chore: add execution evidence` commit (which only adds a result.json evidence
file) rather than the feature commit fcb8a93c referenced in its own
result_rev. The review itself states: "the review should target that commit
or the full branch diff instead."

===========================
Nothing on the code side — the feature is shipped. If anything, the review
tooling's diff-range selection should be fixed so it targets the feature
commit rather than the subsequent evidence chore commit. That is outside
the scope of this bead.

Committing a duplicate re-implementation of code that is already in the base
would be harmful: it would either conflict with the existing files or produce
a no-op commit. Returning no_changes is the correct outcome.

No performance benchmark exists comparing axon backend vs JSONL backend at realistic archive scale (1100 beads). Without this benchmark, the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) cannot evaluate its AC6 gate ('axon parity-or-better than JSONL for ready/blocked/show queue ops').

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/ contains backend_jsonl.go but no axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5, a dep, must land first).
- No benchmark file exercises ready/blocked/show operations on a 1100-bead corpus.
- The missing dep edge (ddx-781a15cf → ddx-95ec5ed5) caused the queue to surface this bead prematurely (dep edge should be added via ).

PROPOSED FIX
- After ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon backend) and ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface) land:
  - Add cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go (or bead_bench_test.go) with:
    - BenchmarkReady_JSONL_1100: ready queue ops on 1100-bead JSONL corpus.
    - BenchmarkReady_Axon_1100: same ops on 1100-bead axon corpus.
    - BenchmarkBlocked_JSONL_1100 and BenchmarkBlocked_Axon_1100.
    - BenchmarkShow_JSONL_1100 and BenchmarkShow_Axon_1100.
  - Generate 1100-bead test corpus (mix of open + closed + archived).
  - Capture wall-time and allocs. Commit results to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/axon-vs-jsonl-benchmark.md.

NON-SCOPE
- axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5).

reason: This bead requires benchmarking the axon backend against the JSONL backend on a 1100-bead corpus, but the axon backend has not yet been implemented. The implementing bead, ddx-95ec5ed5 ("Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"), is still status=open, and there is no axon_backend.go (or equivalent) in cli/internal/bead/ — the only Backend implementation present is backend_jsonl.go. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") are unsatisfiable without that backend.

The bead's dependency DAG was not encoded: per the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) decomposition, this benchmark bead was created with an implicit dependency on ddx-95ec5ed5, but no `ddx bead dep add` edge was recorded, so the queue surfaced this bead as ready prematurely.

Recommended follow-up:
  1. ddx bead dep add ddx-781a15cf ddx-95ec5ed5
     (record the missing edge so the queue stops surfacing this bead until the axon backend lands)
  2. Land ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface refactor, in_progress) and ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon implementation) first.
  3. Re-queue ddx-781a15cf; at that point the benchmark can be written meaningfully against both backends.

Writing a partial benchmark (JSONL only) would satisfy neither AC2 nor AC3 and would mislead the parent epic's AC6 ("parity or better than JSONL") gate.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: The axon bead backend is not yet implemented, so the benchmark this bead asks for cannot be written end-to-end. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") require an axon Backend implementation; none exists in cli/internal/bead/.

Evidence checked:
- `cli/internal/bead/axon/` does not exist (Glob).
- No `BackendAxon` constant, no `axon.New*`, no `NewAxonBackend` references in cli/internal/bead (Grep).
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` exposes only `BackendJSONL`, `BackendBD`, `BackendBR`.
- TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md) is the design spec; it lists the bench (test plan §4) as a Beta gate.
- Sibling beads under parent ddx-5d49b14e show the prerequisite work is still open:
  * ddx-8d747049 open — "axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client"
  * ddx-95ec5ed5 open — "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"
  * ddx-743bc194 open — "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend"
  * ddx-6602559c open — "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" (duplicate of this bead)

Follow-up: this bead should depend on the axon backend implementation beads (ddx-8d747049 and/or ddx-95ec5ed5) and become ready only once those close. Alternatively, dedupe with ddx-6602559c. A jsonl-only benchmark on the 1100-bead corpus could be filed as a separate bead if a baseline is wanted before axon lands, but the AC as written here cannot be satisfied without the axon backend.

reason: The benchmark harness is now present at cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go and the bead package passes `go test ./internal/bead/...`, but the current axon backend still does not meet AC3's parity-or-better requirement on the measured queue ops. done:
- Added backend-specific benchmarks for Ready, Blocked, and Show against JSONL and Axon.
- Built a 1100-bead corpus with active, closed, and archived records.
- Verified `go test ./internal/bead/...` passes.

blocker:
- The axon backend implementation needs further performance work before the benchmark results can satisfy the bead's AC3 parity gate.

follow_up:
- Optimize the axon ReadAll / archive-read path and rerun `go test -run '^$' -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/bead/...` until the benchmark document can honestly record parity-or-better results.

reason: |
  Bead ddx-c9ef6c4f ("Implement axon backend behind selectable backend flag")
  cannot be completed in a single execute-bead pass because two upstream
  prerequisites are not yet in place, and the AC as written requires a
  working end-to-end integration against an external Rust service. Forcing
  a commit here would land non-functional scaffolding (the failure mode
  the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was already BLOCKED for).

  Specific gaps observed in the current worktree (base ab0a056a):

  1. Dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 ("Introduce selectable bead Backend
     interface in cli/internal/bead") is still status=open. The current
     cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines only the legacy interface:

         Backend interface {
             Init() error
             ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
             WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
             WithLock(fn func() error) error
         }

     TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md
     §"Backend interface (Go)") explicitly says this interface is "too
     coarse for Axon — WriteAll(beads []Bead) cannot reasonably be
     implemented against a remote entity store" and prescribes a richer
     per-entity surface (Get/Create/Update with OCC Version,
     AppendEvent, AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch, etc.). Implementing the axon backend against
     the legacy 4-method interface would (a) violate TD-030 and (b)
     produce a backend that cannot honor claim/OCC semantics that the
     bead description and AC #3 require ("plus claim semantics and
     event append"). This bead's <description> says it "follows the TD
     authored in the design child" — that TD assumes the interface
     refactor has landed first.

  2. No Axon Go SDK or generated gRPC client exists in this repo.
     /home/erik/Projects/axon ships axon.proto at
     crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (377 lines, AxonService with
     CreateEntity/GetEntity/UpdateEntity/CommitTransaction/Traverse/
     QueryEntities/PutSchema/CreateCollection/etc.) but no Go bindings.
     TD-030 §"Wire transport" specifies generation via
     protoc-gen-go + protoc-gen-go-grpc into cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/
     with a `make gen-axon-client` target and a checked-in axon.proto
     plus sha256. None of that scaffolding exists; protoc toolchain
     setup, proto vendoring, and the make target are themselves a
     non-trivial chunk of work that should be its own bead.

  3. AC #5 ("ddx bead commands work end-to-end against the axon
     backend on a fresh repo, no migration required") cannot be
     verified inside this worktree. Per TD-030 §D1 the operator runs a
     separate axon-server process; per §D4 the backend refuses all ops
     when Axon is unreachable. There is no Axon server running in this
     execution environment and no fixture / test-double that the AC
     would accept ("end-to-end ... on a fresh repo" rules out an
     in-memory fake).

  Additional context: the parent epic ddx-5d49b14e was BLOCKED in
  post-merge review on 2026-05-02 for landing only execution evidence
  with no implementation, then decomposed into seven children. This
  bead is one of those children but was queued out of dependency order
  — ddx-bbdd7564 (the interface refactor child) needs to land first.

what_is_done:
  - Read TD-030 (the design child's output) end-to-end.
  - Confirmed current Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go
    is the legacy form, not the TD-030 form.
  - Confirmed dependency bead ddx-bbdd7564 is still open.
  - Confirmed no axon proto/client is vendored under cli/internal/bead/.

what_is_blocking:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 must land first to introduce the per-entity Backend
    interface (Get/Create/Update with Version/OCC, AppendEvent,
    AddDep/RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export/Import, error sentinels)
    that this bead's axon implementation needs to satisfy.
  - A separate bead should establish the gRPC client generation
    pipeline: vendor cli/internal/bead/axon/proto/axon.proto from the
    axon repo (with sha256 pin), add the make gen-axon-client target,
    add tooling docs for protoc-gen-go / protoc-gen-go-grpc, and
    commit the generated cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/ tree. This is
    explicitly called out by TD-030 §"Wire transport" but is not in
    the current bead's scope and has no existing bead covering it.
  - AC #5 needs a clarified verification path: either (a) require a
    running Axon test server in CI (with the deployment work that
    implies), or (b) loosen "end-to-end" to mean "round-trip against
    a vendored Axon test harness" and add the harness as a bead.

what_a_followup_would_need:
  - Re-queue this bead (ddx-c9ef6c4f) only after ddx-bbdd7564 closes
    with the TD-030-shaped Backend interface in place.
  - Add (and close) a new prerequisite bead for the axon gRPC client
    generation: vendor proto, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit
    generated stubs under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Add a new prerequisite bead for the Axon test harness used by AC
    #5 (either a test-only docker-compose entry, a `go test`-managed
    server-under-test, or an explicit "skip if AXON_ADDR unset"
    integration-test gate per the project's existing test-tier
    conventions).
  - Then this bead becomes a focused implementation: wrap the
    generated client in cli/internal/bead/axon/, register the
    "axon" backend in cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plumb the
    DDX_BEAD_BACKEND / .ddx/config.yaml selector, and add the
    create/claim/close/dep unit tests AC #4 names (against the
    harness from the new prerequisite bead).
rationale: status: needs_investigation

Why this bead re-queued
=======================
The prior review recorded REVIEW:BLOCK citing "all AC items 1-9" as unmet, but
the review's own notes explain the error: the reviewer examined only the
`chore: add execution evidence` commit (which only adds a result.json evidence
file) rather than the feature commit fcb8a93c referenced in its own
result_rev. The review itself states: "the review should target that commit
or the full branch diff instead."

What a follow-up would need
===========================
Nothing on the code side — the feature is shipped. If anything, the review
tooling's diff-range selection should be fixed so it targets the feature
commit rather than the subsequent evidence chore commit. That is outside
the scope of this bead.

Committing a duplicate re-implementation of code that is already in the base
would be harmful: it would either conflict with the existing files or produce
a no-op commit. Returning no_changes is the correct outcome.

.ddx/executions/20260506T103104-a57adec4/manifest.json:12:    "description": "PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.",

3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.

- beadId, artifactId, parentRunId, childRunIds

- Try-layer fields (inline fragment / nullable): beadId, baseRevision, resultRevision, worktreePath, mergeOutcome, checkResults
- Run-layer fields (inline fragment / nullable): promptSummary, powerMin, powerMax, harness, provider, model, tokensIn, tokensOut, costUsd, durationMs, outputExcerpt, evidenceLinks

GraphQL:
- runs(projectID, layer, beadID, status, harness, first, after): RunConnection
- run(id): Run

The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.",
.ddx/executions/20260506T095008-403be2c1/prompt.md:61:+    "acceptance": "1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.
3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: The axon bead backend is not yet implemented, so the benchmark this bead asks for cannot be written end-to-end. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") require an axon Backend implementation; none exists in cli/internal/bead/.

Evidence checked:
- `cli/internal/bead/axon/` does not exist (Glob).
- No `BackendAxon` constant, no `axon.New*`, no `NewAxonBackend` references in cli/internal/bead (Grep).
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` exposes only `BackendJSONL`, `BackendBD`, `BackendBR`.
- TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md) is the design spec; it lists the bench (test plan §4) as a Beta gate.
- Sibling beads under parent ddx-5d49b14e show the prerequisite work is still open:
  * ddx-8d747049 open — "axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client"
  * ddx-95ec5ed5 open — "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"
  * ddx-743bc194 open — "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend"
  * ddx-6602559c open — "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" (duplicate of this bead)

Follow-up: this bead should depend on the axon backend implementation beads (ddx-8d747049 and/or ddx-95ec5ed5) and become ready only once those close. Alternatively, dedupe with ddx-6602559c. A jsonl-only benchmark on the 1100-bead corpus could be filed as a separate bead if a baseline is wanted before axon lands, but the AC as written here cannot be satisfied without the axon backend.

=======================

the review's own notes explain the error: the reviewer examined only the
`chore: add execution evidence` commit (which only adds a result.json evidence
file) rather than the feature commit fcb8a93c referenced in its own
result_rev. The review itself states: "the review should target that commit
or the full branch diff instead."

What a follow-up would need
===========================
Nothing on the code side — the feature is shipped. If anything, the review
tooling's diff-range selection should be fixed so it targets the feature
commit rather than the subsequent evidence chore commit. That is outside
the scope of this bead.

would be harmful: it would either conflict with the existing files or produce
a no-op commit. Returning no_changes is the correct outcome.",

func TestLoadConfig_BeadBackendField(t *testing.T) {
	tempDir := t.TempDir()

	content := `version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
bead:
  backend: axon
`

	ddxDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(content), 0o644))

	cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(tempDir)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, cfg.Bead)
	assert.Equal(t, "axon", cfg.Bead.Backend)
}
